titleStmt.title: Letter from John Kelly to Sir Robert Chalmers, 6 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Kelly

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Westport,
Co Mayo 8th June 1916. Sir, WESTPORT PETTY SESSIONS 22nd June 1916. I beg to bring the following matter under your notice and to make
a request in connection therewith:— I am Solicitor for the Local Board of Conversators and they
have instructed me to bring a proscution against William Gannon and Austin
Gannon of Drummin, Westport in this County for removing sand from the Salmon
spawning bed of a river at Drummin. The Board regard this as a very bad case and wish me to
write you for the purpose of directing, Mr Hinkson. R.M. at attend at Westport
on the 22nd of June when the case will come on for hearing. I shall arrange with Mr Milling R.M. to attend on that day
also. I shall be much obliged if can accede to my request. Yours faithfully, John Kelly The Under Secretary,
Dublin Castle,
Dublin.
